codeReview.summary: Reviewed feature metadata, handoff, commit eb7bd7d diff, transcript skeleton, and current frontend code. The implementation satisfies the feature intent: Cash App globals theme, sticky navbar, responsive two-column layout with sticky right rail, and placeholder-based section scaffolding that later features have progressively replaced without regressing the layout baseline.

issues.description: Component styling still hardcodes hex colors (and this pattern repeats across layout-related UI files) instead of semantic theme tokens, which conflicts with mission conventions and reduces maintainability.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission/skill guidance clearly says to use semantic tokens (no hardcoded colors outside globals.css), but the feature implementation uses many inline hex values; this indicates convention enforcement is documented but not enforced by tooling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: mission AGENTS.md coding conventions + frontend-ui-worker SKILL.md implementation rules versus code such as web/components/nav.tsx:28,46,52,68 and similar #... classes across placeholder/layout components.

sharedStateObservations.observation: During execution, the declared skill name in feature metadata was not initially invokable, forcing fallback skill resolution.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session 3769e203-a521-45d6-aecf-450b4d52e2af shows Tool Result: "Error: Skill \"frontend-ui-worker\" not found", followed by invocation of "frontend-worker".

summary: Pass. Theme/layout requirements were implemented correctly with no blocking defects in this feature. One non-blocking maintainability issue (hardcoded color usage) remains, plus shared-state observations around convention enforcement and skill-name reliability.
